NAME: External/Removable media
DESCRIPTION: Attack using removable media or peripheral devices.

NAME: Loss or Theft of equipment
DESCRIPTION: Loss or theft of computer devices or media equipment.

NAME: Attrition
DESCRIPTION: Gradually depleting the resources of a target system or network through persistent, repeated cyberattacks, causing service degradation or destruction.

NAME: Web
DESCRIPTION: Attacks targeting websites or web applications.

NAME: Account takeover
DESCRIPTION: Gaining control of a target account to carry out an attack under legitimate credentials.

NAME: Inadequate encryption
DESCRIPTION: Exploiting unencrypted or weakly encrypted data to carry out an attack.

NAME: Browser-based attack
DESCRIPTION: Distributing malicious software through compromised websites.

NAME: Application compromise
DESCRIPTION: Infecting trusted third-party applications with malware to attack users.

NAME: Open port
DESCRIPTION: Leaving a network port accessible and potentially exploitable.

NAME: Vulnerability exploit
DESCRIPTION: Successfully using a vulnerability to gain unauthorized access or control.

NAME: Ransomware
DESCRIPTION: Malicious software that encrypts or locks systems and demands payment for restoration.

NAME: Malware
DESCRIPTION: Software or code designed to damage, disrupt, or gain unauthorized access to a computer system.

NAME: Man in The Middle(MiTM)
DESCRIPTION: Intercepting and manipulating communications between two parties without their knowledge.

NAME: Session hijacking
DESCRIPTION: Taking over an active session, often by stealing session tokens or cookies, to gain unauthorized access.

NAME: Brute-force attack
DESCRIPTION: Attempting all possible password combinations to gain unauthorized access.

NAME: Distributed Denial of Service(DDoS)
DESCRIPTION: Overwhelming a target system or network with massive traffic from multiple compromised systems to disrupt services.

NAME: Watering-hole
DESCRIPTION: Compromising a website frequently visited by a specific group to infect targets with malware.

NAME: Compromised and weak credentials
DESCRIPTION: Using exposed credentials from data breaches or easily guessable passwords to gain access.

NAME: SIM Swap
DESCRIPTION: Transferring a victim’s phone number to a SIM card controlled by an attacker to access accounts and personal data.

NAME: Security misconfigurations and vulnerabilities
DESCRIPTION: Exploiting improper configurations or known security flaws in systems and applications.

NAME: Web forms
DESCRIPTION: Abusing user-input fields on web pages for malicious purposes.

NAME: IP address and IP blocks
DESCRIPTION: Unique addresses assigned to devices (IP addresses) and sets of contiguous addresses (IP blocks). Attackers can scan for open ports or services to exploit.

NAME: Domains and subdomains
DESCRIPTION: Primary domain names and any subordinate domains used by websites.

NAME: Botnet
DESCRIPTION: Two or more compromised computers remotely controlled for malicious activities such as DDoS or TDoS attacks.

NAME: Credential compromise
DESCRIPTION: Stealing authentication credentials (e.g., via phishing or malware) to access systems illegally.

NAME: Telephony Denial of Service(TDoS)
DESCRIPTION: Flooding a telephony network with high call volumes to overwhelm the system and block legitimate calls.

NAME: Replay Attack
DESCRIPTION: Intercepting valid data transmissions and re-sending them to trick a system into granting unauthorized access.

NAME: Cryptanalysis Attacks
DESCRIPTION: Using mathematical techniques to break encryption, exploit weaknesses, or defeat cryptographic security.

NAME: Side Channel Attacks
DESCRIPTION: Extracting sensitive information (e.g., encryption keys) by analyzing physical emissions such as power usage or electromagnetic signals.

NAME: Hardware Trojans
DESCRIPTION: Malicious modifications to hardware or integrated circuits that introduce unintended functionality or vulnerabilities.

NAME: Covert Attacks
DESCRIPTION: Attacks hidden by manipulating normal input/output, evading security detection mechanisms.

NAME: Sleep Deprivation Attack
DESCRIPTION: Repeatedly causing a device to stay active, preventing power-saving modes and potentially leading to system failure.

NAME: Clock Skewing
DESCRIPTION: Altering a system’s timestamps to disrupt time-based validations or synchronize errors for malicious purposes.

NAME: Attacks Targeting System Reputation
DESCRIPTION: Physically tampering with nodes or inserting malware to degrade system reliability and trust.

NAME: Data and Command Integrity Attack
DESCRIPTION: Altering, forging, or deleting system data or commands, undermining data integrity and reliability.

NAME: Eavesdropping
DESCRIPTION: Intercepting and listening to transmitted data or communications without authorization, compromising confidentiality.

NAME: Command and Control Attacks
DESCRIPTION: Remotely sending malicious commands to compromised systems or devices to perform harmful actions.

NAME: Phishing
DESCRIPTION: Attacks carried out via malicious links or attachments leading to fraudulent websites.

NAME: Impersonations/Spoofing
DESCRIPTION: Maliciously masquerading as legitimate content or services.

NAME: Improper usage
DESCRIPTION: Security incidents arising from authorized users violating organizational policies.

NAME: Dumpster diving
DESCRIPTION: Searching discarded materials (trash) for information that can be used to compromise security.

NAME: Advanced Persistent Threat(APT)
DESCRIPTION: Long-term, continuous cyberattacks targeting a specific entity.

NAME: Baiting
DESCRIPTION: Placing an enticing storage device to lure someone into connecting it, thereby infecting their system.

NAME: Shoulder surfing
DESCRIPTION: Secretly observing someone’s credentials or personal data as they are entered.

NAME: Reverse social engineering
DESCRIPTION: After causing a contrived problem, attackers pose as helpers and request sensitive information from the victim.

NAME: Business Email Compromise(BEC)
DESCRIPTION: Attacking corporate email accounts by impersonating executives or business partners.

NAME: Advanced Fee
DESCRIPTION: A scam where a victim pays money upfront expecting something of greater value but receives far less or nothing.

NAME: Extortion
DESCRIPTION: Illegally obtaining money or assets through threats of harm, legal action, or exposing sensitive information.
